info.title: Chainsmith API
info.description: Headless Rust API for the Chainsmith Flesh and Blood deck builder.

parameters.name: classes
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of class names. A card matches if it has any
listed class. Generic cards (no class) match only when the filter is
omitted.

parameters.name: talents
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of talent names. A card matches if it has any
listed talent.

parameters.name: types
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of card type names. A card matches if it has
any listed type.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description:
Filter to cards that are currently `Legal` or `Restricted` in the
given format (snapshot-based, not date-aware — for date-aware
validation use `POST /validate`).

parameters.name: legalForHero
parameters.description:
Card id of a hero. Restricts results to cards whose supertypes are a
subset of the hero's effective supertypes (CR 1.1.3).

200.description: Decks owned by the authenticated user

404.description: Deck not found or not visible to caller

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: ETag from a prior GET/POST/PUT/PATCH response. Required.

404.description: Deck not found or not owned by caller

412.description: If-Match did not match the current deck version

404.description: Deck not found or not owned by caller

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: ETag from a prior GET/POST/PUT/PATCH response. Required.

404.description: Deck not found or not owned by caller

412.description: If-Match did not match the current deck version

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description:
`adult`, `young`, or `pit_fighter`. Multiple values not supported —
most queries want exactly one.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description:
Filter to heroes currently `Legal` in the given format. CC-legal
heroes that have been retired to Living Legend will *not* match
`format=classic_constructed` (their `cc` legality is
`living_legend_retired`); they will match `format=living_legend`.

oneOf.description: Grip on weapon cards. `None` for non-weapons.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor; pass back as `cursor` to fetch the next page. `null`
when there are no more results.

oneOf.description:
A representative printing for catalog tile rendering. The first one
by collector number; `None` when the card has no printings.

name.description: Display name shown to the user (and to other users for public decks).

oneOf.description:
Main-hand slot. Holds a weapon (1H or 2H). A 2H weapon here occupies
both hand slots; see `off_hand` for the Companion exception.

Off-hand slot. Holds either a 1H weapon, an off-hand equipment piece
(shield, Trade Routes, etc.), or — when `main_hand` carries a 2H
weapon — only a Companion-subtyped off-hand equipment piece.

ErrorCode.description:
Stable, machine-readable error codes. Clients are allowed to switch on
these. Adding a variant is additive; renaming or removing one is a
breaking change in production phase (see `.claude/rules/api-contract.md`).

essenceGrants.description:
Class/Talent supertypes the hero grants via "Essence of X" abilities.
These are unioned with the hero's printed supertypes when checking
CR 1.1.3 (supertype subset rule).

weaponZoneCount.description:
Number of weapon zones the hero has — i.e. the maximum number of
`CardType::Weapon` printings the loadout may equip across main and
off-hand combined. Default 2 (the standard hero). Kayo variants
override to 1; Bolfar to 0. Non-weapon equipment in the off-hand slot
(shields, Companion items, etc.) does not consume a weapon zone.

HeroMoniker.description:
The first comma-separated token of a hero's name (e.g. "Bravo, Star of the
Show" -> "Bravo"). FaB specialization rules match on moniker, not full name.

Keyword.description:
Free-form keyword. Upstream prints new keywords every set, so an enum here
would be brittle and is not needed for legality checks (the validator does
not switch on keyword).

LegalitySummary.description:
Snapshot of per-format legality from the upstream `card.json` flat booleans.
Convenience for catalog filtering only — the validator is the source of
truth for legality at a specific date.

PatchDeckRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /decks/{id}` — updates a subset of metadata fields. Any
field omitted is left unchanged. To clear `description` back to absent,
use `PUT` (PATCH cannot represent the difference between "not changing"
and "setting to null" without doubling the field's nesting).

imageUrl.description:
CDN URL for the card image, sourced from upstream. Clients (web,
iOS, Android) load this URL directly from the LSS CDN — we do not
proxy or cache imagery on our infrastructure (see
`.claude/rules/security.md`).

ReplaceDeckRequest.description:
Body for `PUT /decks/{id}` — replaces the entire deck (metadata + pool +
loadouts) atomically. Use this for normal "save my deck" flows where the
client edits in memory and writes the whole thing back.

date.description:
Validation date. Used to evaluate time-evolving rules (banned list,
Living Legend retirement). Defaults to today UTC if omitted.

ViolationCode.description:
Stable codes clients are allowed to switch on.

Adding a variant is additive. Renaming or removing one is a breaking
change in the production phase (see `.claude/rules/api-contract.md`).
